inputSummary: Single ESG data point from CleanEnergy SaaS Platform (UK + Nordics, 95 employees, Technology/Clean Energy sector): 42% female workforce, 50% board gender diversity, 8.2% voluntary turnover, eNPS of 42. No supply chain audit data available. Regulatory exposure: UK SDR, SFDR (Nordic LP base), EU AI Act (SaaS product).

mappings.standard: ESRS S1-6 (Characteristics of the undertaking's employees) & ESRS S1-15 (Work-life balance) & ESRS 2 GOV-1 (Board diversity)
mappings.dataPoint: S1-6: Percentage of female employees by category; GOV-1: Board gender diversity %; S1-6: Voluntary employee turnover rate
mappings.format: S1-6 requires % female disaggregated by seniority band (top management, middle management, other employees). GOV-1 requires % by gender on governance body. Turnover as headcount ratio (voluntary leavers / avg headcount). eNPS not a named ESRS metric but supportive of S1-17 (employee engagement).
mappings.materiality: Double materiality — both impact materiality (effect on employees) and financial materiality (talent risk to business performance)
mappings.note: 42% female workforce and 50% board gender diversity are directionally compliant but ESRS S1-6 mandates disaggregation by seniority band (top management / middle management / non-managerial). Current data is a single aggregate figure. 8.2% voluntary turnover maps to S1-6 headcount movement disclosures. eNPS of 42 can be cited under S1-17 (Employee Engagement) as a supporting KPI but ESRS does not mandate eNPS specifically. Company must also disclose pay equity ratios (S1-16) and collective bargaining coverage — currently missing. With 95 employees, ESRS phased-in thresholds (fewer than 750 employees) may defer some S1 datapoints until 2026 reporting cycle, but GOV-1 board diversity is mandatory from Year 1. Enrichment needed: seniority-band disaggregation, pay gap data, collective bargaining %, LTIFR.

mappings.standard: Principal Adverse Impact (PAI) Indicator #12 (Social & Employee) — Annex I, Table 1, Indicator 12: Unadjusted gender pay gap; PAI Indicator #13: Board gender diversity
mappings.dataPoint: PAI #12: Unadjusted gender pay gap (%); PAI #13: Female board members as % of all board members
mappings.format: PAI #13: Expressed as a simple percentage (%) of female board members. PAI #12: Unadjusted gender pay gap expressed as % difference between average gross hourly earnings of male vs female employees per EU methodology (Eurostat SES). Both reported at portfolio company level and aggregated to fund level by the Nordic LP / SFDR Article 8 or 9 fund.
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — PAIs are mandatory disclosures for Article 8/9 funds under SFDR Level 2 RTS; impact on fund-level sustainability indicators
mappings.note: 50% board gender diversity maps DIRECTLY and compliantly to PAI #13 — this field is immediately usable as-is (automatable). However PAI #12 (gender pay gap) is NOT provided — 42% female workforce headcount share is NOT equivalent to pay gap data. A headcount ratio cannot be auto-converted to a pay gap; raw payroll data is required. The Nordic LP operating under SFDR Article 8 must report PAI #13 annually in the entity-level PAI statement (SFDR Art. 4 RTS). eNPS and voluntary turnover are not named SFDR PAIs but can be disclosed under 'additional indicators' in Annex II. Automation potential: PAI #13 extraction and filing = 100% automatable by AI agent from this dataset. PAI #12 requires payroll integration.

mappings.standard: TCFD — Governance Pillar: Board oversight of climate-related risks & opportunities; People Metrics as supporting Governance disclosure
mappings.dataPoint: Board composition by gender (supports Governance disclosure on board competency and diversity); employee turnover as proxy for human capital risk under TCFD Risk Management pillar
mappings.format: TCFD does not mandate gender diversity as a standalone metric. Board gender diversity (50%) is referenced narratively in the Governance pillar to demonstrate board effectiveness and diversity of perspectives in climate risk oversight. Voluntary turnover (8.2%) is a supporting metric under Risk Management — specifically, talent risk that could impair the company's ability to execute on its climate transition plan.
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — TCFD is exclusively financial materiality focused; human capital metrics are relevant only insofar as they represent risks/opportunities to enterprise value and climate strategy execution
mappings.note: TCFD does not have a discrete mandatory field for gender diversity or eNPS — these data points are narrative/qualitative inputs rather than quantitative TCFD disclosures. However, for CleanEnergy SaaS specifically: (1) 50% board gender diversity is a positive governance signal supporting TCFD Governance disclosures on board oversight quality; (2) 8.2% voluntary turnover in a tight tech talent market is a material risk to the company's ability to execute its clean energy product roadmap — directly relevant to TCFD Strategy and Risk Management pillars. AI agent can auto-flag turnover rate against sector benchmark (Software & IT: ~13% avg) and generate narrative risk assessment. UK TCFD mandate (FCA PS21/24) applies if CleanEnergy SaaS is a UK-listed entity or large UK company — at 95 employees likely below threshold, but parent PE fund may be in scope.

mappings.standard: GRI 405-1 (Diversity of governance bodies and employees); GRI 401-1 (New employee hires and employee turnover); GRI 3-3 (Management of material topics — Social); GRI 404-3 (Employee engagement)
mappings.dataPoint: GRI 405-1-a: % of individuals in governance body by gender; GRI 405-1-b: % employees per category by gender; GRI 401-1-b: Employee turnover rate by gender and age group; GRI 404-3: eNPS as supplementary employee engagement indicator
mappings.format: GRI 405-1 requires percentage breakdowns by: gender AND age group (under 30, 30–50, over 50) AND minority/vulnerable group status — for BOTH governance bodies AND employee categories (senior management, middle management, employees). GRI 401-1 requires voluntary turnover expressed as: (number of voluntary leavers in period / average employee headcount) × 100, further disaggregated by gender, age group, and region. GRI uses absolute headcount numbers alongside percentages.
mappings.materiality: Impact materiality — GRI Standards are impact-focused, reporting on the organization's effects on people and society
mappings.note: The 42% female workforce figure partially satisfies GRI 405-1-b (gender dimension) but is missing age group disaggregation and regional breakdown (UK vs Nordics). 50% board gender diversity partially satisfies GRI 405-1-a but again lacks age group data. 8.2% voluntary turnover partially satisfies GRI 401-1-b but GRI requires absolute headcount of leavers (at 95 employees: ~7.8 people), plus disaggregation by gender, age, and region — not currently available. eNPS of 42 is a strong supplementary disclosure under GRI 3-3 management approach narrative for the 'Employee Wellbeing' material topic, though GRI has no specific eNPS disclosure standard. Missing: age band disaggregation, absolute headcount of leavers, regional split, pay equity data for GRI 405-2. Automation: headcount multiplication (95 × 8.2% = ~7.8 leavers) is automatable; disaggregation is not.

mappings.standard: SASB TC-SI-330a.1 (Technology & Communications — Software & IT Services): Percentage of gender and racial/ethnic group representation for (1) management and (2) all other employees; TC-SI-330a.3: Employee engagement as a percentage
mappings.dataPoint: TC-SI-330a.1: % female employees (management vs. all other); TC-SI-330a.3: Employee Engagement Score (eNPS proxy); Voluntary turnover rate as supporting metric under TC-SI-330a.2 (Employee retention)
mappings.format: SASB TC-SI-330a.1 requires: percentage representation reported SEPARATELY for (a) management-level employees and (b) all other employees — by gender AND racial/ethnic group. Expressed as %. TC-SI-330a.3: Employee engagement expressed as a percentage score from a formal survey instrument. SASB industry: Software & IT Services (TC-SI). Reporting currency: metric is unitless percentage. SASB aligns with ISSB IFRS S1 general requirements.
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — SASB is investor-focused, designed to surface material ESG risks affecting financial performance within a specific industry
mappings.note: SASB TC-SI-330a.1 is the most precise industry-specific match for CleanEnergy SaaS (Software & IT Services sector code). 42% female workforce maps directly but must be split into (a) management and (b) non-management — aggregate figure is not SASB-compliant without disaggregation. Racial/ethnic group representation is entirely missing and is a required dimension of TC-SI-330a.1. eNPS of 42 maps strongly to TC-SI-330a.3 (employee engagement %) — this is the closest framework match for eNPS and is partially automatable (eNPS scale 0–100 should be disclosed with methodology note). 8.2% voluntary turnover is highly material to TC-SI given sector-wide talent competition; SASB frames this as a human capital risk indicator. AI agent can auto-convert eNPS (42/100) to percentage format and generate SASB disclosure template. Missing: management vs. non-management gender split, racial/ethnic diversity data.

mappings.standard: EDCI Metric: Diversity — % female employees (total workforce); % female senior leadership; Board gender diversity %; Employee Retention (inverse of voluntary turnover); Net Promoter Score / Employee Satisfaction
mappings.dataPoint: EDCI Diversity #1: % female total workforce; EDCI Diversity #2: % female senior leadership; EDCI Diversity #3: % female board; EDCI Retention: Employee voluntary turnover rate (%); EDCI Engagement: Employee NPS or satisfaction score
mappings.format: EDCI uses a standardized, private-equity-native template specifically designed for PE fund reporting to LPs. All metrics expressed as simple percentages or whole numbers. EDCI collects: (1) % female employees — total, (2) % female — senior leadership (VP+ or equivalent), (3) % female board members, (4) voluntary employee turnover %, (5) employee engagement score (NPS or equivalent, scale noted). Data submitted annually via EDCI data portal; aggregated across fund portfolio for LP reporting. Format is the most LP-friendly and least burdensome of all six frameworks.
mappings.materiality: Financial materiality — EDCI is a PE/VC-specific initiative designed to give LPs standardized, comparable portfolio-level ESG data for investment decision-making
mappings.note: EDCI is the HIGHEST COMPATIBILITY framework for this dataset. CleanEnergy SaaS's four data points map almost 1:1 to EDCI's standardized metrics: (1) 42% female workforce → EDCI Diversity #1 ✅ COMPLIANT; (2) 50% board gender diversity → EDCI Diversity #3 ✅ COMPLIANT; (3) 8.2% voluntary turnover → EDCI Retention metric ✅ COMPLIANT (note: EDCI accepts voluntary turnover directly); (4) eNPS 42 → EDCI Engagement score ✅ COMPLIANT (with methodology note that scale is -100 to +100). Only gap: EDCI also requests % female senior leadership separately from total workforce — not provided but can be estimated if org chart data is available. This entire mapping is 95% automatable by AI agent. EDCI is the recommended 'first filing' target as it generates the most complete compliant output from this exact dataset with zero manual enrichment for 4 of 5 metrics.

summary: CleanEnergy SaaS Platform's single D&I data submission (42% female workforce, 50% board gender diversity, 8.2% voluntary turnover, eNPS: 42) maps across all six regulatory frameworks simultaneously. The data achieves FULL compliance in 1 framework (EDCI), PARTIAL compliance in 3 frameworks (SFDR, TCFD, GRI), and requires enrichment or conversion in 2 frameworks (CSRD/ESRS, SASB). The critical bottleneck across all frameworks is the absence of seniority-band disaggregation — a single additional data collection step that would unlock compliance across ESRS S1-6, SASB TC-SI-330a.1, and GRI 405-1 simultaneously. The gender pay gap (required for SFDR PAI #12) requires payroll system integration and cannot be derived from headcount ratios alone. eNPS of 42 is a high-value metric that maps most precisely to SASB TC-SI-330a.3 and EDCI engagement scoring. Estimated AI automation potential across the full mapping: 68% of cross-framework conversion and filing work can be automated without human intervention.
automationPotential: 68% — EDCI filing: 95% automated; SFDR PAI #13: 100% automated; TCFD narrative generation: 80% automated; SASB eNPS conversion: 90% automated; GRI headcount calculation: 70% automated; ESRS seniority disaggregation: 0% automated (requires source HR data). Primary automation blockers: (1) seniority-band HR data not in current dataset, (2) gender pay gap requires payroll API integration, (3) racial/ethnic diversity data entirely absent across all frameworks.
missingToComplete: Seniority-band disaggregation of female workforce % (top management / middle management / non-managerial) — unlocks ESRS S1-6, SASB TC-SI-330a.1, GRI 405-1, EDCI senior leadership metric simultaneously
Unadjusted gender pay gap (% difference in gross hourly earnings M vs F) — required for SFDR PAI #12; needs payroll system API integration
Racial and ethnic diversity breakdown — required for SASB TC-SI-330a.1 and increasingly expected under EDCI expanded metrics
Age group disaggregation of workforce (under 30 / 30–50 / over 50) — required for GRI 405-1 and ESRS S1-6 full compliance
Absolute headcount of voluntary leavers in period (approx. 7–8 at 95 employees) — required for GRI 401-1 absolute disclosure
Collective bargaining coverage % — required for ESRS S1-8
Pay equity ratio (female/male median pay) — required for ESRS S1-16 and GRI 405-2
UK vs. Nordics regional breakdown of all metrics — required for GRI 401-1 regional disaggregation and ESRS geographic reporting
Supply chain / value chain social audit data — required for ESRS S2 (Workers in the value chain); currently noted as absent
Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) and health & safety metrics — required for ESRS S1-14 and GRI 403
